design_philosophy.core_loop: Accelerate with A, manage turbo boost with B, time lane shifts and air balance on jumps to maintain momentum through obstacles and track segments. Repeat across multiple races.
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Turbo boost heat management vs. speed gain; landing physics (balanced wheels = momentum preservation vs. poor landing = speed loss/crash); competitive race positioning requiring consistency.
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: High-speed motocross racing with precise control over bike physics and momentum, creating a sense of skill mastery through landing technique and thermal management.
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: The game emphasizes landing precision and momentum preservation over pure speed. Poor landings actively punish the player with speed loss, teaching deliberate control. Turbo is a high-risk resource (overheating forces cooldown), not a guaranteed advantage. Design Mode allows player-created content, positioning the game as both consumable experience and creative tool.

difficulty.continues_system: Multiple race attempts available; must finish 3rd or higher in preliminary races to qualify for championship
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Selection A (solo race) vs. Selection B (vs. computer opponents) provide two difficulty modes. Design Mode enables custom track creation with variable difficulty. Computer-controlled racers provide escalating competitive pressure.

progression.unlock_mechanism: Must qualify for championship races by finishing 3rd or higher in preliminary races. Championship races unlock as player progresses.
replayability_hooks: Design Mode with 19 types of hurdles enables unlimited custom track creation
Save/load functionality for custom tracks (Famicom Data Recorder, later restored in N64/3DS versions)
Two selection modes (solo vs. competitive) with different challenge structures
VS. system arcade variant with three difficulty levels

world_building_techniques: Track naming/course progression implies motocross competition structure
Visual distinction between preliminary and championship races

environmental_storytelling: Course obstacles (gaps, rough patches, lane bottlenecks) escalate difficulty progressively
Temperature/thermal feedback system (heat arrows reset turbo overheat state)
Lane system (four lanes standard, bottlenecks reduce to two) creates dynamic racing pressure

sources_used: wikipedia - Excitebike gameplay mechanics, selection modes, design mode details, progression structure
tcrf - regional differences, save/load functionality across platforms
